verses.text: On the first day of the seventh month ye shall have a holy convocation, no servile work — it is a day of blowing the trumpets. Offer one bullock, one ram, seven lambs without spot, with meat and drink offerings, and one kid for a sin offering. Beside the new moon and the continual offering. Guidance: The seventh month opens with a sky split by trumpets. No work — just a call to attention. The sound says it's time to listen, time to gather, time to remember whose year this is.

On the tenth day of the seventh month ye shall have a holy convocation; afflict your souls, do no work. Offer one bullock, one ram, seven lambs without blemish; one kid for a sin offering, beside the sin offering of atonement and the continual burnt offering. Guidance: Israel's holiest day arrives quiet, not loud. Afflict your souls — fast, sit low, name the sin out loud. The high priest enters the holy of holies today. The cost of being God's people, paid in advance every year.

On the fifteenth of the seventh month, a holy convocation — the feast of tabernacles, seven days. Day one: thirteen bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs, with their meat and drink, plus a sin offering. Each day decreasing: twelve, eleven, ten, nine, eight, seven. Same rams and lambs throughout. Guidance: The lavish feast that decreases. Seventy bullocks across the week — one for every nation in Genesis 10. Israel's harvest joy poured out for the world. The descending count makes room — the feast that ends gentler than it began.

On the eighth day a solemn assembly, no servile work. Offer one bullock, one ram, seven lambs without blemish, with meat and drink offerings, plus one kid for a sin offering. Guidance: After seven days of abundance, an eighth day quiet and small. The volume drops. One bullock instead of seven. The feast ends not with a bigger party but with a stiller assembly — the people lingering with God before going home. The best meal is the one that doesn't rush to clear the table.

These ye shall do unto the LORD in your set feasts, beside your vows and freewill offerings, your burnt offerings, meat offerings, drink offerings, peace offerings. Moses told Israel all that the LORD commanded him. Guidance: The required is the floor, not the ceiling. Vows, freewill gifts, peace offerings — the heart that goes beyond the calendar is the heart God wants. The set feasts are bones; the freewill is the breath. Worship calendars give shape; love gives life.
